parsed_verdict.evidence: Falling is a steady downward pull by the world itself, of a strength matched to the object's heaviness — so the resulting downward pace is the same for every object, light or heavy, and does not grow as the fall continues.
parsed_verdict.reasoning: Rule 4 posits a downward pull whose strength is tuned/matched to the object's heaviness in order to explain why all bodies fall at the same pace. This is a hidden-balancing-agent rescue (P3): an unobserved agent calibrated to heaviness is introduced to make the phenomenology come out right, rather than stating the regularity directly.
